article_title: Star Fox Returns June 25 on Switch 2 as a Velan Studios Remake of Star Fox 64, the First New Entry in About a Decade
summary: Chief Editor override of automated REJECT: the sole error is a false-positive orphan-link flag. The Wikipedia URL contains parentheses, so the orphan-detection regex truncated it at the inner ')'; the full URL with both parens IS in article.sources with a committed snapshot (source-2) and is fully verifiable. All four sources read and confirmed; original topic. APPROVE.

findings.message: Body cites 1 URL(s) not in article.sources (provenance break)
findings.details:
These URLs appear as Markdown links in body_markdown but are missing from article.sources, so they have no committed snapshot under sources/YYYY-MM/<slug>/ and cannot be verified through the provenance chain. Orphan URL(s):
  - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Star_Fox_(2026_video_game

content_preview.title: Star Fox Returns June 25 on Switch 2 as a Velan Studios Remake of Star Fox 64, the First New Entry in About a Decade
content_preview.summary: Nintendo's first new Star Fox in roughly ten years is a Velan Studios remake of Star Fox 64, launching June 25 exclusively on Switch 2 with mouse-aim controls and 4-vs-4 Battle Mode.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Nintendo's Star Fox series is returning after roughly a decade away, with a new entry built on the 1997 Nintendo 64 classic. According to [Nintendo](https://www.nintendo.com/us/whatsnew/star-fox-direct-sees-fox-mccloud-and-crew-prepare-for-liftoff-on-nintendo-switch-2-june-25/), the game launches on Nintendo Switch 2 on June 25 and is based on the Star Fox 64 game with a complete visual overhaul. [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Star_Fox_(2026_video_game)) describes the tit...

recommendations: Close the PR without merging. The work is discarded under the v3.9.0 workflow.
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

Either add the orphan URL(s) to article.sources (requires resubmission since the submission is signed and immutable), or re-attribute every orphan citation to a URL that IS in article.sources, then resubmit.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (561 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Clear Overview / What We Know / Pricing / What We Don't Know sections, neutral tone, every claim attributed inline to a specific source.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 source snapshots read from disk and verified. source-0 (nintendo.com, 200): confirms June 25 launch, $49.99 regular price, Joy-Con 2 mouse controls with 'more intuitive aiming', Campaign/Challenge/4-vs-4 Battle modes, Battle stages (zone control on Corneria, energy crystals on Fichina, cargo from space pirates on Sector Y), private matches/matchmaking, co-op 'gunner duties while you focus on flying' via GameShare/GameChat, 'GameShare online is only available for Nintendo Switch 2', Andross, complete visual overhaul, based on Star Fox 64. source-1 (nintendolife.com, 200): confirms Velan Studios revealed on Bluesky, second Nintendo collaboration after Mario Kart Live: Home Circuit (2020), made Knockout City, digital $49.99/£41.99 and physical $59.99/£49.99, demo with tutorial + 'one of the game's opening stages'. source-2 (en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Star_Fox_(2026_video_game), 200): correct page; confirms 'remake of Star Fox 64 (1997), it expands on the original game's level design, story, and gameplay mechanics', developed by Velan Studios / published by Nintendo, exclusive to Switch 2, Nintendo 64 Wireless Controller supported, surprise Nintendo Direct on May 7, 2026 announced ten minutes before broadcast. source-3 (nintendolife.com, 200): confirms exact quote 'brand new looks for Fox, Falco, Peppy, and Slippy, in order to make them look a little more animalistic', prologue focusing on Fox's father James McCloud, 'cinematic take', unannounced Star Fox Direct via Nintendo Today app with broadcast ~ten minutes later.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every number, date, name, and direct quote traces verbatim to a cited snapshot. The two quoted phrases ('more intuitive aiming' from Nintendo; the Fox/Falco/Peppy/Slippy 'animalistic' line from Nintendo Life) appear verbatim in their snapshots. The article's 'standard controls' phrasing for Wikipedia is a defensible paraphrase of the implied default Joy-Con/button controls the page describes alongside mouse mode and the N64 Wireless Controller. No hallucinations detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No sensationalism, no editorializing, no AI self-reference. The 'What We Don't Know' section responsibly flags the minor reveal-date inconsistency between sources rather than papering over it.
editor_notes.originality: Original topic. No prior Star Fox or Velan Studios coverage in the archive. Distinct from the June 12 Zelda: Ocarina of Time Switch 2 remake article, though both are Switch 2 remakes of N64 Nintendo classics (linked as related_articles in the meta file).
concerns: The automated chief:review script auto-set verdict REJECT on a single 'Source attribution / provenance break' error. This is a confirmed FALSE POSITIVE: the orphan-detection regex /\]\((https?:\/\/[^)]+)\)/ stops at the first ')', so for the Markdown link [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Star_Fox_(2026_video_game)) it captures the truncated 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Star_Fox_(2026_video_game' (missing the inner closing paren). The true URL 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Star_Fox_(2026_video_game)' (with both parens) IS present in article.sources and DID receive a committed snapshot at source-2.html.gz (status 200, sha256 recorded in manifest.json). The provenance chain is intact; nothing is unverifiable. As Chief Editor I override the automated REJECT to APPROVE.

The 'Sources not in allowlist' warning for nintendo.com is benign: nintendo.com is the official first-party publisher of the game and the most authoritative source for the announcement; it is reputable and HTTPS.
recommendations: Tracking item (not blocking this article): the orphan-link regex in scripts/chief_editor_review.ts should be hardened to handle balanced parentheses inside URLs (e.g. Wikipedia article titles), so parenthesized source URLs do not trigger spurious provenance-break REJECTs.
Consider adding nintendo.com to config/source_allowlist.txt as a recognized first-party publisher.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, well-sourced News submission. The single automated error is a verified false positive caused by parentheses in the Wikipedia URL; the full URL is correctly in article.sources with a committed, verifiable snapshot. All claims confirmed against the source snapshots. APPROVE.
